description: An ACDP error envelope whose optional 'error.details' field is JSON null. acdp-error.schema.json types 'details' as an object ("type": "object"), NOT as a nullable type. Per the absent-vs-null wire convention (RFC-ACDP-0005 §2.2.1), an error with no structured details MUST OMIT 'details', never serialize it as null. A registry MUST NOT emit 'error.details': null; a strict receiver MUST reject it as a non-conformant wire shape.

expected.registry_emit_behavior: A registry MUST NOT emit 'error.details': null. An error with no structured detail object omits the 'details' key entirely.
expected.rationale: error.details is an optional, non-nullable object. The conformant wire form for 'no details' is an omitted key, not an explicit null. A receiver that coerces null to absent special-cases the wire format and diverges from the single unambiguous form the convention mandates; a strict receiver treats 'details': null as a parse error.
